rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL VS. MARK BELVEDERE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Arbitrator ---Ccp Section 1281.6]
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, February 2, 2017, LINE 1 PLAINTIFF MICHAEL CARROLL'S Motion For Appointment Of Arbitrator, CCP Section 1281.6 GRANTED. No opposition was filed. The Court appoints Ret. Judge Ernest Goldsmith as the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Carroll is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/RU

rulings.Case Title: JUVIN FLORES VILLANUEVA VS. EL TREBOL BAR, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Deeming Admitted The Truth Of Matters Specified In Request For Admissions, Set One; And Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Juvin Flores Villanueva
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEB. 02, 2017, LINE 1, DEFENDANT EL TREBOL BAR INC.'S Motion For An Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Deeming Admitted The Truth Of Matters Specified In Request For Admissions, Set One; And Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Juvin Flores Villanueva Pro Tem Judge Diane Peebles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Having duly considered the moving papers and no opposition thereto the court issues the following tentative ruling to Defendant El Trebol Bar Inc.'s Motion for An Order Compelling Answers to Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Deeming Admitted The Truth of Matters Specified in Request For Admissions, Set One, and Awarding Sanctions against Juvin Flores Villanueva: the motion is hereby GRANTED. Plaintiff Juvin Flores Villanueva shall serve verified Responses to Form Interrogatories Set Number One within 20 days of this order. All objections to Form Interrogatories have been waived. The truth of the matters specified in the Request for Admissions Set Number One are deemed admitted. The court grants sanctions against Juvin Flores Villanueva under CCP 2033.280 in the amount of $390.00 to be paid to the moving party within 30 days of this order. No sanctions are awarded pursuant to CCP 2030.290 as the moving party did not meet and confer with the Plaintiff prior to filing this motion. The moving party shall prepare the Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dpeebleslaw@comcast.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: ***Transferred to Santa Clara County**JONATHAN OOMRIGAR VS. TIBCO SOFTWARE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, February 2, 2017, LINE 9 DEFENDANT TIBCO SOFTWARE SINGAPORE PTE LTD.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS DENIED. At a minimum, the hiring process for plaintiff's position with the moving defendant - a focus of many of his claims - was conducted in California. The service of process was valid.
